card_description: Dionysos (Greek: "Zeus of Nysa") is a divine or mythological figure appearing in Philip K. Dick's Exegesis. In the classical tradition, Dionysos is god of wine and ecstasy; dying and rising deity. PKD connects Dionysos to the dying-and-rising god pattern. Appears in 10 Exegesis passages. Associated with dictionary terms: Dionysos. Context: "Dick identifies "Possession by God" (Dionysian mystery) as the secret core of Christianity, rediscovered by Meister Eckhart."
